content.problemStatement:
Write a program using `while` loop to check if the positive number NNN is prime or not.

**Note:** Prime numbers are the numbers that are divisible by only 1 and the number itself.


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain a single positive number NNN.


Output Format

Output on a single line, `YES` if NNN is prime, else `NO`.
content.inputFormat: The first and only line of input will contain a single positive number N.
content.outputFormat: Output on a single line, `YES` if N is prime, else `NO`.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 11 has no divisor other than 1 and itself → prime.

solution.approach: The key insight is to check divisibility from 2 to the square root of N. If N is divisible by any number in this range, it is not a prime number. We use a while loop to check for divisibility.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space used does not grow with the size of the input.
